assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 contains: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified tests/api/purl_recommend.rs: -1 test function removed (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers), +1 test function added (test_recommend_purls_dedup), assertions updated in test_recommend_purls_basic. New file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs: +3 test functions added. Removal is semantically justified (qualifier behavior no longer exists), but structurally reductive signals are present alongside additive ones. |'

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Reductive findings' section states: 'tests/api/purl_recommend.rs: test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers removed -- this test verified qualifier-specific behavior that is no longer part of the API contract. Removal is justified by the feature change, but it represents a structural reductive signal.' Also the structural summary lists '-1 test function (test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers)'.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: The structural summary in report.md mentions '+2 assertions in test_recommend_purls_basic (added !contains(?) checks)' and the Test Requirements Detail says 'assertion updated to check pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12 and added !contains(?) checks'. However, neither the structural summary nor the reductive findings identify this as a RELAXED assertion — i.e., that the original assertion checked a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers and was weakened to check a versioned PURL without qualifiers. The reductive findings section only lists the removed test function. The assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic is framed as meeting a requirement (PASS), not as a reductive signal or relaxation.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Test Change Classification Detail' section states: 'New file: tests/api/purl_simplify.rs — +3 test functions, +11 assertions (purely additive)'. Also the Test Change Classification row mentions 'New file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs: +3 test functions added.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md structural summary states: '+1 test function (test_recommend_purls_dedup)'. The Test Change Classification row also states '+1 test function added (test_recommend_purls_dedup)'. The Test Requirements Detail confirms: 'Add test_recommend_purls_dedup to verify deduplication — PASS — new test function added'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are verification/report documents (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The report presents analysis of diffs and tests but contains no indication of modifying source code, running write operations on code files, or executing code-altering commands. The outputs are purely analytical.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and detailed findings but does not execute any merge operation. The footer states 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr', indicating it is presented as a review comment for human consumption. No merge commands, merge API calls, or auto-merge actions are present in any output file.
